meta.title: The Airstream Archive
meta.tagline: Every era of America's silver icon — models, floor plans, lengths, photos & advertising since 1931
meta.imageSource: Photographs are embedded from Wikimedia Commons via stable Special:FilePath URLs. Each is freely licensed (Creative Commons or public domain); credit and a link back to the source file appear on every image. Vintage advertisement slots link to public archives where verified, copyright-clear scans can be dropped in.
meta.note: Specifications reflect the best publicly documented figures for each model line. Airstream frequently offered several lengths and floor plans within a single model name and year, and trim/options changed across a model's run. Lengths and floor plans are representative of the model line unless a specific year is noted.

milestones.1931: Wally Byam builds and sells his first trailers — the Torpedo Car Cruiser — from a Los Angeles backyard, and publishes build-it-yourself plans.
milestones.1936: The riveted-aluminum Clipper debuts, establishing the aircraft-inspired monocoque silhouette every Airstream has followed since.
milestones.1948: Airstream resumes and expands postwar production, riding the great American road-trip boom.
milestones.1952: Airstream opens its Jackson Center, Ohio factory — still the company's home today.
milestones.1954: Airstream and the Bowen Water Heater Co. introduce the first hot-water system offered in a travel trailer.
milestones.1958: Airstream introduces the first fully self-contained travel trailer, freeing owners from campground hookups.
milestones.1961: The 16-foot Bambi — the smallest and most beloved of the classic Airstreams — is introduced.
milestones.1969: Airstream builds the Mobile Quarantine Facility that isolates the Apollo 11 astronauts after their return from the Moon.
milestones.1972: The value-priced, painted Argosy line launches at a new plant in Versailles, Ohio.
milestones.1974: Airstream builds its first motorhome, applying its aluminum craft to a self-propelled land yacht.
milestones.1979: The Argosy travel-trailer line is discontinued.
milestones.1990: The Classic name debuts atop the range as Airstream's flagship trailer.
milestones.2004: The Mercedes-Benz Sprinter-based Interstate touring coach launches Airstream's modern Class B program.
milestones.2016: The rugged, go-anywhere Basecamp arrives, aimed at a new outdoors-first generation.
milestones.2020: The Bambi name returns on Airstream's modern compact line, alongside the revived Caravel.

funFacts.torpedo-car-cruiser: Byam first sold the Torpedo as a set of mail-order plans in his own magazine before he ever built one for sale.
funFacts.silver-cloud-airlite: These transitional models bridged the gap between Byam's wooden Torpedo and the all-aluminum Clipper that made Airstream famous.
funFacts.clipper: The Clipper's aircraft-style aluminum body was engineered with Hawley Bowlus — the man who built the fuselage of Lindbergh's Spirit of St. Louis.
funFacts.wanderer-liner-postwar: Byam spent WWII building aircraft parts, then brought those aluminum techniques straight back to his postwar trailers.
funFacts.flying-cloud-classic-era: The Flying Cloud borrows its name from a famous 1851 clipper ship — and proved so beloved that Airstream revived it in 2009.
funFacts.cruisette-safari-early: The compact Cruisette previewed the tow-anywhere idea that the legendary Bambi would perfect a decade later.
funFacts.safari: The Safari's name honored Wally Byam's international caravans, which took Airstreams from Central America to the length of Africa.
funFacts.overlander: The 26-foot Overlander was Byam's own favorite size for going the distance on his round-the-world caravans.
funFacts.tradewind: The Trade Wind name returned in 2024 on Airstream's first factory solar-and-lithium, go-anywhere off-grid trailer.
funFacts.globetrotter-classic: The Globe Trotter name was reborn in 2018 for a premium, European-styled interior.
funFacts.bambi-original: 'Bambi' means 'little one' — and today the original 16-footer is among the most collectible trailers on earth.
funFacts.caravel: The reborn Caravel is famous for bringing polished-aluminum accents inside the cabin, not just outside.
funFacts.ambassador: The 28-foot Ambassador debuted with Airstream's deluxe 'International' appointments.
funFacts.sovereign: Airstream billed the flagship Sovereign as the 'Sovereign of the Road.'
funFacts.international-land-yacht: The romantic 'Land Yacht' nickname of these years still shapes how people picture a classic Airstream.
funFacts.argosy: Argosys were painted — usually beige — so Airstream could use aluminum panels that weren't show-polished, at a friendlier price.
funFacts.excella: The Excella 500 was the best-appointed Airstream money could buy in its day.
funFacts.airstream-motorhome-70s: In 1974 Airstream put its aluminum craft on wheels of its own, building its first motorhome.
funFacts.classic-limited: Today's Classic pairs 1990s flagship heritage with heated floors, panoramic windows and a smart-control system.
funFacts.land-yacht-motorhome: The Land Yacht badge carried Airstream's motorhomes from the late '70s clear into the 2000s.
funFacts.bambi-international-ccd: Designer Christopher C. Deam reinvented the Airstream interior for the 21st century with these models.
funFacts.interstate: The Interstate is built on a Mercedes-Benz Sprinter — it drives like a van but lives like a yacht.
funFacts.flying-cloud-modern: The modern Flying Cloud offers more lengths and floor plans than any other Airstream — it's the brand's best-seller.
funFacts.basecamp: The Basecamp's big rear hatch swallows bikes and gear; there's even a special edition co-designed with REI.
funFacts.globetrotter-modern: The reborn Globetrotter leans into European design, slotting between the everyday Flying Cloud and the flagship Classic.
funFacts.caravel-bambi-modern: The revived Bambi and Caravel share identical floor plans — the Caravel just wears more premium finishes.
funFacts.atlas-rangeline: The Atlas hides a Murphy bed that folds away to open up the whole cabin by day.
funFacts.trade-wind-modern: The 2024 Trade Wind is Airstream's most off-grid-ready trailer, built around a big solar-and-lithium system.

tradewind/1962.file: 1962 Airstream Tradewind custom gold 24 foot travel trailer during 2019 Vintage Camper Trailer Rally at Cam-Plex in Campbell County, Wyoming (1).jpg
tradewind/1962.caption: A 1962 24-ft Trade Wind in custom gold anodized finish

tradewind/1962.file: 1962 Airstream Tradewind custom gold 24 foot travel trailer during 2019 Vintage Camper Trailer Rally at Cam-Plex in Campbell County, Wyoming (2).jpg
tradewind/1962.caption: 1962 Trade Wind — rear view

overlander/1963.file: 1963 Airstream trailer in Joshua Tree dllu.jpg
overlander/1963.caption: A 1963 Airstream in Joshua Tree

meta.marketNote: Weights, rarity and values are approximate and vary widely with length, options and condition — treat them as ballpark, and always verify weights against the trailer's own data plate.

torpedo-car-cruiser.why: Very few 1930s Torpedoes are known to survive.

clipper.why: The original 1936–41 Clipper is a holy grail for collectors.

safari.why: The 22-ft Safari is one of the most desirable vintage lengths.

overlander.why: Built in good numbers; a full-timing favorite.

globetrotter-classic.why: Shorter classic bodies are less common.

bambi-original.why: The 1961–63 16-footer is among the most collectible trailers ever made.
bambi-original.production: Only a few hundred built across 1961–63

caravel.why: Compact self-contained charm keeps demand high.

ambassador.why: Larger deluxe bodies survive in moderate numbers.

sovereign.why: The flagship length was built in quantity.

international-land-yacht.why: 'International' spanned several lengths and commands a premium.

argosy.why: Painted bodies make survivors stand out.

excella.why: Built in good numbers into the 1980s.

airstream-motorhome-70s.why: Aluminum Class A survivors are prized.

classic-limited.why: In production; the top of the range.

land-yacht-motorhome.why: 1980s–2000s Class A coaches turn up occasionally.

bambi-international-ccd.why: Plenty circulate on the used market.

flying-cloud-modern.why: In production; the most common modern Airstream.

trade-wind-modern.why: In production; the solar/lithium flagship trailer.

serial.where: Look for a riveted aluminum data plate — historically inside near the entry door, with the serial also stamped on the A-frame (tongue) at the front. Modern trailers add a 17-character VIN on the frame and door jamb.
serial.reading: Vintage plates list the model, length, serial and often the build month/year. From 1981 on, U.S. trailers use a standardized 17-digit VIN whose 10th character encodes the model year. Pre-1981 numbers were sequential and model-specific, so a title or club registry is the surest way to confirm an exact year.
serial.tip: The WBCCI and vintage registries can often match a serial number to its build year.

community.label: Vintage Airstream Club
community.note: The Airstream Club's chapter for pre-1980 trailers.

community.label: Vintage Airstream
community.note: Restoration knowledge base and ad archive.

eras.name: The Founding Era
eras.blurb: Wally Byam builds his first trailers in a Los Angeles backyard, patents the 'Clipper', and pioneers the riveted-aluminum monocoque that defines Airstream forever.

eras.name: Postwar Boom
eras.blurb: Production resumes after WWII. Airstream perfects the self-contained trailer, opens an Ohio plant, and launches the model names — Flying Cloud, Safari, Overlander — that still run today.

eras.name: The Golden Age
eras.blurb: The Caravan years. Wally Byam's international caravans make Airstream a legend, the little Bambi arrives, and the lineup swells from the 16-ft Bambi to the 31-ft Sovereign.

eras.name: Land Yacht Seventies
eras.blurb: A second plant, the painted Argosy line, the first Airstream motorhome, and the top-trim Excella. Big, comfortable, chrome-and-gold-anodized touring trailers.

eras.name: Modern Classic
eras.blurb: The Classic name debuts atop the range, the Land Yacht motorhome matures, and designer Christopher Deam reinvents the interior for a new century with the International CCD.

eras.name: The Revival
eras.blurb: A design-led renaissance: reborn Bambi and Caravel, the go-anywhere Basecamp, the Globetrotter, Mercedes-based touring coaches, and smart-tech Classics.

models.name: Torpedo Car Cruiser
floorplans: Single open cabin with fold-down bunks and a small galley
highlights: Airstream's first named product
Sold as both finished trailers and mail-order plans
Masonite-and-wood body on a light chassis
models.description: The trailer that started it all. In 1931 Wally Byam began selling the Torpedo Car Cruiser — first as build-it-yourself plans in his magazine, then as finished units from a small Los Angeles shop. Named for its streamlined, teardrop-influenced shell, the Torpedo established Byam's obsession with light weight, low drag and 'a place for everything.' By 1932 more than a thousand were reportedly on the road.

ads.note: Byam advertised the Torpedo through his own 'Trailer Travel' magazine and mail-order plan listings.

models.name: Silver Cloud & Airlite
floorplans: Front cabin sleeping, mid galley, rear storage
highlights: Transitional models between the wooden Torpedo and the aluminum Clipper
Larger, more comfortable cabins
Steps toward true streamlining
models.description: Between the pioneering Torpedo and the landmark Clipper, Byam offered larger models such as the Silver Cloud and Airlite. They kept the composite construction of the era but grew roomier and more aerodynamic, bridging the gap to the all-aluminum design that would soon make Airstream famous.

ads.note: Early Airstream promotion appeared in trailer-travel and automotive magazines of the mid-1930s.

models.name: Clipper
floorplans: Front dinette convertible to bed, center galley, enclosed water supply, rear berth
highlights: First riveted-aluminum monocoque Airstream
Named after the Pan Am Clipper flying boats
Aircraft-style construction: light, strong, aerodynamic
Water supply, electric lights, an enclosed galley and dry-ice air cooling
models.description: The 1936 Clipper is the trailer that made Airstream an icon. Inspired by aircraft designer Hawley Bowlus (of Spirit of St. Louis fame) and refined by Byam, its riveted aluminum-alloy skin over a semi-monocoque frame was radically light and strong. It carried its own water, electric lighting, an enclosed galley and even a rudimentary dry-ice cooling system, and it looked like nothing else on the road. Every Airstream since traces its silhouette to the Clipper.

ads.note: The Clipper was advertised as 'the aristocrat of trailers' — original brochures survive in collector archives.

models.name: Wanderer / Liner
floorplans: Front bedroom, center dinette & galley, rear bath area (early self-contained experiments)
highlights: First postwar Airstreams as production resumed in 1945
Refined the rounded 'Bubble' body
Groundwork for the fully self-contained trailer
models.description: Airstream halted trailer production during WWII (Byam worked in aircraft manufacturing). When peace returned, the company resumed with rounded, aluminum 'Bubble' trailers under names like Wanderer and Liner, selling into the exploding postwar road-trip market and setting the stage for the self-contained revolution of the late 1950s.

images.caption: A restored 1949 Airstream showing the postwar 'Bubble' body

ads.note: Postwar ads emphasized freedom, family travel and 'living out of doors in comfort.'

models.name: Flying Cloud
floorplans: Front bedroom or convertible sofa, center galley & dinette, rear bath
highlights: One of Airstream's longest-lived model names
Mid-size, easy-towing family trailer
Named for a 19th-century clipper ship
models.description: Introduced in the late 1940s, the Flying Cloud became the friendly, mid-size heart of the Airstream line — big enough for a family, light enough to tow behind the sedans of the day. The name proved so enduring that Airstream revived it in 2009, and it remains a cornerstone model to this day.

ads.note: 1950s ads pitched the Flying Cloud to young families as an affordable path into Airstream ownership.

models.name: Cruisette
floorplans: Compact front dinette/bed, single-side galley
highlights: Airstream's compact entry of the mid-1950s
Precursor to the small-trailer idea later perfected by the Bambi
models.description: The Cruisette answered demand for a smaller, more affordable Airstream that a light family car could tow easily. Its compact footprint foreshadowed the beloved 16-ft Bambi that would arrive at the start of the 1960s.

images.caption: A compact vintage Airstream travel trailer

ads.note: Marketed on ease of towing and low price of entry.

models.name: Safari
floorplans: Front bedroom, center dinette & galley, rear bath (self-contained)
highlights: Named for Wally Byam's legendary caravans
The quintessential mid-size Airstream of the 1960s
22-ft length ideal for couples and small families
models.description: Few names capture Airstream's spirit like Safari — a nod to Wally Byam's globe-spanning caravans. The 22-ft Safari became one of the most popular trailers of the 1960s: self-contained, easy to tow, and perfectly sized for adventure. Airstream revived the Safari name decades later for a new generation.

images.caption: An Airstream Safari on the road

images.file: Airstream Safari, Old Town AIexandria waterfront.jpg
images.caption: A Safari parked on the Old Town Alexandria waterfront

ads.note: Safari advertising leaned into the romance of the Wally Byam Caravan Club and world travel.

models.name: Overlander
floorplans: Front bedroom, center bath, rear dinette/lounge & galley — or twin/double bed variants
highlights: Airstream's big-family touring trailer
Named for the trans-continental 'overland' caravans
A favorite for extended and full-time travel
models.description: The 26-ft Overlander was built for going the distance — Byam himself favored larger trailers for his international caravans. With room for a family and full self-contained systems, the Overlander became the go-to for long-haul and full-time Airstreamers through the 1960s and 70s.

images.file: 1963 Airstream trailer in Joshua Tree dllu.jpg
images.caption: A 1963 Airstream in Joshua Tree — the long-body touring era

ads.note: Ads framed the Overlander as the trailer for those who 'live in it, not just vacation in it.'

models.name: Trade Wind
floorplans: Front bedroom, center bath & galley, rear lounge/dinette
highlights: The versatile 24-ft 'in-between' size
Bridged the Safari and Overlander
Revived in 2024 as an off-grid-capable trailer
models.description: Slotting neatly between the 22-ft Safari and 26-ft Overlander, the 24-ft Trade Wind offered a sweet spot of livability and towability. The name returned in 2024 on a modern, solar-and-lithium off-grid Airstream — a fitting tribute to a classic.

images.file: 1962 Airstream Tradewind custom gold 24 foot travel trailer during 2019 Vintage Camper Trailer Rally at Cam-Plex in Campbell County, Wyoming (1).jpg
images.caption: A 1962 24-ft Trade Wind in custom gold anodized finish

images.file: 1962 Airstream Tradewind custom gold 24 foot travel trailer during 2019 Vintage Camper Trailer Rally at Cam-Plex in Campbell County, Wyoming (2).jpg
images.caption: 1962 Trade Wind — rear view

ads.note: Promoted as the ideal all-around family length.

models.name: Globe Trotter
floorplans: Front dinette/bed, center galley, rear bedroom or twin beds
highlights: Compact-to-mid international traveler
Name revived in 2018 as a premium European-styled model
models.description: The original Globe Trotter was a compact-to-mid-size trailer aimed at the wandering spirit its name implies. Airstream brought the Globetrotter name back in 2018 for a premium, European-influenced trim with a distinctive modern interior.

images.caption: A classic polished Airstream travel trailer

ads.note: Named to evoke worldwide travel and the caravan lifestyle.

models.name: Bambi
floorplans: Rear dinette/bed, mid galley & bath, front storage — famously compact single-room layout
highlights: The smallest classic Airstream — an icon of cuteness
Highly collectible today
Inspired the modern small-trailer revival
models.description: The original 16-ft Bambi (1961–1963) is the most beloved of all small Airstreams — light enough for a modest car, endlessly charming, and today among the most sought-after vintage trailers in existence. Its name was resurrected in the 2000s and again in 2020 for Airstream's modern compact line.

ads.note: The Bambi was pitched at couples and first-time buyers wanting the Airstream look in a tow-anywhere size.

models.name: Caravel
floorplans: Front gaucho/dinette bed, center galley & bath, rear beds
highlights: Compact touring trailer with full self-contained systems
Name revived in 2019 for the modern polished-interior line
models.description: The 1960s Caravel packed genuine self-contained capability into a compact, easily towed body. Prized by vintage collectors for its balance of size and features, the Caravel name returned in 2019 on a modern model celebrated for its retro-polished aluminum interior accents.

ads.note: Sold on the promise of full trailer comforts in a compact package.

models.name: Ambassador
floorplans: Front bedroom, center bath, rear lounge & galley; twin or double bed options
highlights: Introduced with the International-grade deluxe trim
Large touring trailer just below the flagship Sovereign
models.description: The 28-ft Ambassador debuted in 1963 as part of Airstream's move upmarket, offering generous space and deluxe International-series appointments. It sat near the top of the range, just under the flagship Sovereign, for buyers who wanted room to roam in style.

images.caption: An Airstream International-series trailer, the deluxe trim shared by the Ambassador

ads.note: Positioned as a luxury long-body tourer.

models.name: Sovereign
floorplans: Front bedroom, center bath, rear lounge/dinette & galley — the classic 'Sovereign of the Road' layout
highlights: The flagship full-length Airstream of the era
Marketed as the 'Sovereign of the Road'
Roomy enough for extended and full-time living
models.description: The Sovereign was the era's flagship — a full 30–31 ft of polished aluminum billed as the 'Sovereign of the Road.' With a proper bedroom, central bath and a spacious rear lounge, it was the trailer for those who wanted the most Airstream money could buy.

images.caption: A long-body Airstream in International/Sovereign configuration

ads.note: Advertised as the top of the line — the ultimate land yacht of its day.

models.name: International (Land Yacht)
floorplans: Deluxe versions of Safari, Overlander and Sovereign floor plans
highlights: Top-tier 'International' trim across multiple model lengths
Upgraded appointments, appliances and finishes
'Land Yacht' became the era's nickname for the whole range
models.description: 'International' was Airstream's premium trim designation, applied across the model lengths to denote upgraded appliances, finishes and features — the difference between a fine trailer and a truly deluxe one. The romantic 'Land Yacht' nickname of these years still colors how people picture a classic Airstream.

images.caption: A polished classic Airstream in touring trim

ads.note: The International trim was advertised as the connoisseur's Airstream.

models.name: Argosy
floorplans: Front or rear bedroom variants with center bath and galley
highlights: Painted (usually beige) instead of polished — hid non-cosmetic aluminum
Built at the Versailles, Ohio plant
Distinctive rounded 'wrap' front and rear windows on some models
A more affordable way into an Airstream-built trailer
models.description: Argosy was Airstream's clever value line: built on the same principles but painted (typically beige or 'sandstone') so it could use aluminum panels without cosmetic-grade polish. Produced in Versailles, Ohio from 1972, Argosy trailers — and later motorhomes — offered the Airstream build at a friendlier price. The line wound down at the end of the decade, making surviving examples a distinctive collector favorite.

images.caption: A classic Airstream-built travel trailer of the 1970s

ads.note: Argosy ads highlighted Airstream engineering at a more accessible price.

models.name: Excella
floorplans: Rear bedroom or rear bath, center galley & dinette, front lounge
highlights: Top-of-the-line trim from the mid-70s into the 80s
Deluxe finishes, appliances and upholstery
Often seen with the wide 'Excella' script bands
models.description: The Excella took over as Airstream's premium trim in the mid-1970s and carried the flag into the 1980s — the best-appointed trailer in the catalog, with upgraded galleys, finishes and comforts. An Excella 500 of this era is a benchmark 'classic modern' Airstream.

images.caption: A well-kept later polished Airstream touring trailer

ads.note: Advertised as the finest Airstream of its day.

models.name: Airstream Motorhome (Class A)
floorplans: Front cab & lounge, center galley & bath, rear bedroom — self-propelled land yacht
highlights: Airstream's first motorhome (1974)
Riveted-aluminum Class A body
Foundation of the later Land Yacht motorhome line
models.description: In 1974 Airstream applied its aluminum craft to a self-propelled 'land yacht,' launching its first motorhome. These riveted-aluminum Class A coaches carried the Airstream look under their own power and led directly to the Land Yacht motorhomes of the following decades.

images.caption: An Airstream Class A motorhome (310 series)

ads.note: Marketed as the self-propelled Airstream land yacht.

models.name: Classic / Limited
floorplans: Rear bedroom, center bath(s), front lounge & galley — the top-appointed layouts
highlights: The flagship model name from 1990 to today
Largest and most luxurious travel trailers Airstream makes
Modern versions add smart-tech, heated floors and premium finishes
models.description: The Classic (and its 'Limited' predecessor) has anchored the top of the Airstream range since 1990. These are the largest, most luxurious silver trailers in the catalog — and today's Classic pairs that heritage with a Smart Control System, panoramic windows, and hotel-grade appointments.

images.caption: A large, modern polished Airstream trailer
A modern Airstream in polished aluminum

ads.note: Positioned as the ultimate expression of the Airstream trailer.

models.name: Land Yacht Motorhome
floorplans: Front cab & lounge, central galley/bath, rear bedroom or twin-bed layouts
highlights: The definitive Airstream Class A of the 80s–2000s
Aluminum and later composite bodies
Also spawned Argosy and Legacy motorhome variants
models.description: The Land Yacht name carried Airstream's motorhome program from the late 1970s through the 2000s — comfortable, well-built Class A coaches that brought the touring-in-comfort ethos to the driver's seat. Related badges (Argosy, Legacy) appeared over the years as the line evolved.

ads.note: Advertised as the motorized Airstream land yacht.

models.name: Bambi / International CCD
floorplans: Modernist open plans by designer Christopher Deam — rear bath or rear bed, streamlined galley
highlights: Revived the small Airstream idea for the 2000s
Christopher C. Deam (CCD) redesigned the interior for a modern era
Bridged vintage charm and contemporary design
models.description: In the 2000s Airstream reconnected with its small-trailer roots, reviving the Bambi name and commissioning designer Christopher C. Deam to modernize the interior. The resulting International CCD models — clean, bright and contemporary — reintroduced Airstream to a new design-conscious generation.

ads.note: Marketed on modern design and the reborn Airstream lifestyle.

models.name: Interstate (Touring Coach)
floorplans: Front cab lounge, mid galley & seating, rear bed/bath on a Mercedes-Benz Sprinter chassis
highlights: Built on the Mercedes-Benz Sprinter van
Airstream's luxury Class B touring coach
Drives like a van, lives like a yacht
models.description: The Interstate brought Airstream into the modern touring-coach era: a luxury Class B motorhome built on the Mercedes-Benz Sprinter chassis, with a full galley and bath in a package easy enough to daily-drive. It established the premium van-life segment Airstream still leads.

ads.note: Advertised on Mercedes-Benz refinement paired with Airstream craft.

models.name: Flying Cloud (Modern)
floorplans: Front or rear bedroom, bunk options, family-friendly center layouts — the most floor-plan choices in the range
highlights: The best-selling modern Airstream
The widest choice of lengths and floor plans
Family and bunk layouts available
models.description: Airstream revived the Flying Cloud in 2009, and it has since become the brand's versatile best-seller — offered in the widest span of lengths and floor plans, including family-friendly bunk layouts. It's the model most buyers choose as their first modern Airstream.

images.file: Airstream travel trailer, Lakolk beach, Rømø island, Denmark (2024-08-19).jpg
images.caption: A modern Airstream travel trailer at the beach

images.file: Airstream caravan by the pier on Furillen 1.jpg
images.caption: A modern Airstream by the pier on Furillen, Sweden

ads.note: The modern Flying Cloud is marketed as the do-everything Airstream.

models.name: Basecamp
floorplans: Convertible rear dinette/bed, wet bath, rear hatch for gear — adventure/toy-hauler style
highlights: Airstream's rugged, go-anywhere small trailer
Wide rear hatch swallows bikes and gear
Lightweight, aerodynamic, off-grid-capable
models.description: The Basecamp reimagined the small Airstream as an adventure machine: light, aerodynamic and tough, with a big rear cargo hatch for bikes, boards and gear, plus a wet bath and convertible sleeping. It's the model that draws outdoors-first buyers who might never have considered a silver trailer.

ads.note: Marketed to outdoor enthusiasts; sold in REI and other special editions.

models.name: Globetrotter (Modern)
floorplans: Twin or queen rear bed, central bath, front lounge — European-influenced interiors
highlights: Premium, European-styled interior design
Distinctive materials and finishes
Slots between Flying Cloud and Classic
models.description: The reborn Globetrotter (2018) is Airstream's design-forward premium trailer, with European-influenced interiors, richer materials and a more curated aesthetic than the everyday Flying Cloud — for buyers who want extra polish without stepping up to the flagship Classic.

images.file: Airstream caravan by the pier on Furillen 3.jpg
images.caption: A premium modern Airstream trailer

ads.note: Advertised on refined, European-inspired design.

models.name: Caravel & Bambi (Modern)
floorplans: Shared 16/19/20/22-ft layouts — rear bath or rear bed, compact galley; Caravel adds polished-aluminum interior accents
highlights: Reborn compact classics (Bambi 2020, Caravel 2019)
Identical floor plans; Caravel is the more premium trim
The easy-tow gateway into modern Airstream ownership
models.description: Airstream relaunched two beloved small-trailer names for a new era: the Caravel (2019) and Bambi (2020). They share the same tidy 16-, 19-, 20- and 22-ft floor plans, with the Caravel carrying more premium finishes and signature polished-aluminum interior accents. Together they're the friendliest way into a modern Airstream.

ads.note: Marketed as heritage names reborn for modern adventurers.

models.name: Atlas & Rangeline (Touring Coaches)
floorplans: Atlas: Murphy-bed suite, full bath, rear lounge (B+); Rangeline: modern galley, convertible seating, wet/dry bath
highlights: Atlas (2017): luxury B+ with a slide-out Murphy suite
Rangeline (2023): reimagined modern touring coach
Both on the Mercedes-Benz Sprinter platform
models.description: At the top of Airstream's motorized range sit the Atlas — a luxury Class B+ with a clever Murphy-bed suite that opens the floor by day — and the Rangeline, a 2023 ground-up touring coach with a modern galley and flexible living space. Both build on the Mercedes-Benz Sprinter for road refinement.

ads.note: Advertised as Airstream's most luxurious way to drive and camp.

models.name: Trade Wind (Modern Off-Grid)
floorplans: Rear or front bedroom, central bath, front lounge — built around a large solar + lithium off-grid system
highlights: Revives the classic Trade Wind name (2024)
Factory solar and lithium battery bank for boondocking
Airstream's most off-grid-capable trailer
models.description: In 2024 Airstream brought back the Trade Wind name on its most off-grid-ready trailer yet — a factory package of expanded solar, a large lithium battery bank and efficient systems built for days away from hookups. A modern nod to the caravanning spirit of the original.

images.file: Airstream caravan by the pier on Furillen 5.jpg
images.caption: A modern Airstream trailer in a remote setting

ads.note: Marketed on solar/lithium off-grid capability.
